ucs.title: [LLM] TA413 (LuckyCat / Tropic Trooper) FriarFox malicious Firefox extension drop
ucs.description: Hunts non-browser/non-installer processes writing XPI files or content into a Firefox profile's extensions directory — TA413's FriarFox add-on (delivered via fake Adobe Flash update redirect) used this exact persistence path to harvest Gmail session/cookie data from Tibetan diaspora targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(Filesystem.file_hash) as file_hash values(Filesystem.process_path) as process_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path="*\\Mozilla\\Firefox\\Profiles\\*" AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\extensions\\*" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.xpi") AND Filesystem.process_name!="firefox.exe" AND Filesystem.process_name!="msiexec.exe" AND Filesystem.process_name!="setup.exe" AND Filesystem.process_name!="OUTLOOK.EXE" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | convert ctime(firstSeen) ctime(lastSeen) | where firstSeen=lastSeen OR firstSeen!=lastSeen
ucs.kql:
// TA413 / FriarFox — sideloaded Firefox add-on drop
let _legit_writers = dynamic(["firefox.exe","msiexec.exe","setup.exe","setup_wizard.exe","OfficeClickToRun.exe"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where FolderPath has @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\"
| where FolderPath has @"\extensions\" or FileName endswith ".xpi" or FileName =~ "extensions.json" or FileName =~ "addons.json"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_legit_writers)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
// pull any same-host browser-credential reads in the same 1h window — escalates the alert
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType == "FileOpened" or ActionType == "FileModified"
    | where FileName in~ ("cookies.sqlite","key4.db","logins.json","places.sqlite","sessionstore.jsonlz4")
    | project DeviceId, CredReadTime = Timestamp, CredFile = FileName, CredReader = InitiatingProcessFileName
  ) on DeviceId
| where isempty(CredReadTime) or (CredReadTime between (Timestamp - 1h .. Timestamp + 1h))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          Dropper = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          DropperPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          DropperSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          XpiName = FileName, XpiPath = FolderPath, XpiSHA256 = SHA256,
          CredFile, CredReader, CredReadTime
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// TA413 / FriarFox — sideloaded Firefox add-on drop (ASIM portable)
let _legit_writers = dynamic(["firefox.exe","msiexec.exe","setup.exe","OfficeClickToRun.exe"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where TargetFilePath has @"\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\"
| where TargetFilePath has @"\extensions\" or TargetFileName endswith ".xpi" or TargetFileName in~ ("extensions.json","addons.json")
| extend ActingBin = tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where ActingBin !in~ (_legit_writers)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          Dropper = ActingProcessName, DropperCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine,
          XpiPath = TargetFilePath, XpiName = TargetFileName, XpiSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: TA413 FriarFox Malicious Firefox Extension Drop
id: 8e2c3a47-1d6f-4f1a-9b8e-2a4413414fox
status: experimental
description: Detects a non-browser, non-installer process writing XPI files or contents into a Mozilla Firefox profile extensions directory. Aligns with TA413 (Tropic Trooper / LuckyCat / Pirate Panda) FriarFox tradecraft used to steal Gmail session/cookie data from Tibetan diaspora targets.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0081/
  - https://www.proofpoint.com/us/blog/threat-insight/ta413-leverages-new-friarfox-browser-extension-target-gmail-accounts-global
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1176
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1539
  - attack.t1555.003
  - attack.g0081
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_profile:
    TargetFilename|contains: '\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles\'
  selection_artifact:
    - TargetFilename|contains: '\extensions\'
    - TargetFilename|endswith: '.xpi'
    - TargetFilename|endswith: '\extensions.json'
    - TargetFilename|endswith: '\addons.json'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\setup.exe'
      - '\OfficeClickToRun.exe'
  condition: selection_profile and selection_artifact and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Enterprise extension deployment via GPO or Configuration Manager (rare; allowlist by signing cert)
  - Manual sideload of internal extension by IT
level: high
ucs.rationale: FriarFox (revealed Feb 2021 by Proofpoint) is a TA413-specific tooling artefact — no other public APT cluster has been reported deploying it. The install vector is the high-fidelity choke point: legitimate Firefox extension installs always have firefox.exe as the writer (or msiexec for enterprise GPO). A standalone PE writing into the profile's extensions dir is the exact behavioural signature TA4
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] TA413 Royal Road RTF: Equation Editor (CVE-2017-11882) child-process spawn
ucs.description: Hunts any child process spawned by eqnedt32.exe — TA413 has consistently used the Chinese-shared 'Royal Road' / '8.t' RTF weaponizer that exploits the Equation Editor (CVE-2017-11882 / CVE-2018-0802) to drop Sepulcher, ExileRAT, and LOWZERO loaders, often with template-injection chained to remote payload retrieval.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as child_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="eqnedt32.exe" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstSeen) ctime(lastSeen) | join type=left dest [ | tstats summariesonly=true count as net_count values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as c2_ip values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2_host from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.app="winword.exe" OR All_Traffic.app="excel.exe" OR All_Traffic.app="powerpnt.exe" OR All_Traffic.app="eqnedt32.exe") AND NOT (All_Traffic.dest_ip=10.0.0.0/8 OR All_Traffic.dest_ip=172.16.0.0/12 OR All_Traffic.dest_ip=192.168.0.0/16) by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as dest ]
ucs.kql:
// TA413 / Royal Road — Equation Editor child spawn + Office remote-template fetch
let _suspect_window = 30d;
let EqEditChildren = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(_suspect_window)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "eqnedt32.exe"
    | project EqTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ChildImage = FolderPath, ChildName = FileName,
              ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine, ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
              GrandparentDoc = InitiatingProcessParentFileName;
// Pair with Office-process egress to a public IP within 5 minutes — Royal Road template / 2nd stage pull
let OfficeEgress = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(_suspect_window)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","eqnedt32.exe")
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where not(ipv4_is_in_any_range(RemoteIP, dynamic(["23.0.0.0/8","104.16.0.0/13"])))   // strip CDN noise; tune per env
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl,
              EgressProc = InitiatingProcessFileName;
EqEditChildren
| join kind=leftouter OfficeEgress on DeviceId
| where isempty(NetTime) or NetTime between (EqTime - 5m .. EqTime + 5m)
| project EqTime, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ChildName, ChildImage, ChildCmd, ChildSHA256,
          GrandparentDoc, NetTime, EgressProc, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl
| order by EqTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// TA413 / Royal Road — Equation Editor child spawn (Sentinel SecurityEvent 4688)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\eqnedt32.exe"
| where AccountType == "User"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          Child = NewProcessName, ChildCmd = CommandLine,
          Parent = ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: TA413 Royal Road RTF - Equation Editor Spawning Child Process
id: 4b1d8f02-5c33-49a1-8fae-413royalroadrtf
status: stable
description: Microsoft Equation Editor (eqnedt32.exe) spawning any child process is a near-binary indicator of CVE-2017-11882 / CVE-2018-0802 exploitation. TA413 (Tropic Trooper / LuckyCat / Pirate Panda / KeyBoy) consistently uses the shared Chinese 'Royal Road' / '8.t' RTF weaponizer to deliver Sepulcher, ExileRAT, and LOWZERO via this exact chain.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0081/
  - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2017-11882
  - https://www.anomali.com/blog/royal-road-re-dive-resurfacing-8-t-dropper
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1221
  - attack.g0081
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\eqnedt32.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Equation Editor invocation does not spawn child processes; any hit warrants triage
level: high
ucs.rationale: eqnedt32.exe is an in-process COM server for embedded equations — under normal use it never spawns a child process. Any child spawn is essentially diagnostic of an exploit primitive (CVE-2017-11882 / CVE-2018-0802). TA413 has been documented across Proofpoint, Recorded Future (Insikt), and Citizen Lab reporting using Royal Road / 8.t RTF builders that chain this exploit with remote template inject
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
